classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: It is put in the Retractations immediately after the De Actis cum Felice Manichæo, which was written about the end of the year 404.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: It is one of the most argumentative of the Anti-Manichæan treatises, and so one of the most abstruse and difficult.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: The lines of argument here pursued have already been employed in part in the earlier treatises.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: The most interesting portions of the contents of the treatise, and the most damaging to the Manichæans, are the long extracts from Mani’s Thesaurus, and his Fundamental Epistle.—A.H.N.
